This is the first route that plausibly satisfies all four gates at once after integration. The “topoelectrical” half is already good at intrinsic boundary and corner localization with direct boundary readout; the “superconducting microwave” half is already good at coherent tunability and coupled-mode transport. The missing piece is the hybrid integration, not the individual ingredients. [Nature+5Nature+5Nature+5](https://www.nature.com/articles/s42005-018-0035-2?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

## 1. Primitive medium

A CRBSM is a scaffolded cellular medium $M$ with three native ingredients.

### (a) Bipolar shell phase

Each cavity $v$ has a thin shell phase with two stable boundary polarities

$$\sigma_v\in\{+,-\}.$$

The two poles are intrinsic and physically distinguished by the shell itself, not by external labels.

This is the residue atom:

$$Gr_+(1,2)\rightsquigarrow \sigma_v=\pm.$$

### (b) Anchored boundary sheets

Low-energy excitations are not volumetric states but **sheets**

$$\Sigma \subset M$$

separating opposite shell polarities. These sheets anchor to selected scaffold ribs. Inside one cavity, the admissible anchored sheet families define an ordered orthogonal frame

$$f_1,\dots,f_m.$$

This is the intrinsic root-bundle layer.

### (c) Chiral relay pockets

For each adjacent pair $(f_i,f_{i+1})$, the scaffold contains a twisted saddle chamber $R_i$.  
A relay pocket is geometrically biased so that the only low-energy sheet transition through it is

$$(f_i,f_{i+1})\mapsto(f_{i+1},-f_i).$$

So the local physics of the relay pocket **is** the quarter-turn operator

$$J_i.$$

2600: That filter immediately selects **factorization/gluing regimes** and rejects two broad classes. It rejects regimes whose “boundary” is merely chosen by an observer, and it rejects regimes where the full boundary/readout data reconstructs the bulk faithfully. So the mathematics points first to **scattering systems**, then to **cut-and-glue topological field regimes**, then to **coherent inverse-scattering media**. It points away from ordinary interface-designated computation, and it also points away from any regime where the inverse problem is too faithful. 2602: ## Best current fit: asymptotic scattering systems
2603: 
2604: The strongest mathematics-first hit is **asymptotic scattering in local field theories and coherent wave systems with genuine in/out channels**. Here the carrier is the legal asymptotic state space subject to on-shell and conservation constraints; the canonical readout is the scattering map or amplitude; and that readout is recursively organized by poles, cuts, unitarity, and factorization. Modern on-shell reviews explicitly describe amplitudes as being determined from their poles and cuts using unitarity and factorization, and recent dispersive work constructs nonperturbative amplitudes by iterating unitarity from spectral input. That is the closest physical realization of $S2^\*$ I know: the semantic object is not a chosen output register but the canonical scattering data attached to the intrinsic asymptotic boundary of the process. [Annual Reviews+2ScienceDirect+2](https://www.annualreviews.org/content/journals/10.1146/annurev.nucl.012809.104538?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
2605: 
2606: This regime also gives the cleanest physical realization of $S4$. In other words, asymptotic scattering already realizes “same canonical boundary/readout, different bulk description” in a native and nontrivial way. If I take the specification seriously, this is the first physical regime to look at: **relativistic multiparticle scattering in local gauge, scalar, and gravitational field theories**, and more generally any coherent scattering medium whose native semantics is carried by asymptotic channels and factorization data. [ScienceDirect+2ScienceDirect+2](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0029558263907314?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
